message:
feat: implement high-assurance promotion, tiered trust storage, policy explainer, retroactive hardening, integrity sweep, counterfactual replay, and proof-carrying repair subsystems

Connector layer:
- high_assurance_promotion.rs: Adds a multi-gate promotion pipeline
  for elevating connector trust level from standard to high-assurance.
  Requires passing cryptographic attestation, behavioral history audit,
  and policy-explainer sign-off before promotion is granted. Rejected
  promotions are logged with denial reasons for audit.
- tiered_trust_storage.rs: Implements a tiered storage authority map
  where data durability guarantees scale with the trust level of the
  owning connector. Higher-trust connectors get stronger replication
  and integrity-check guarantees.
- root_pointer.rs: Extended to support epoch-scoped root bootstrap
  validation, ensuring root pointers from stale configuration epochs
  are rejected fail-closed.

Policy layer:
- policy_explainer.rs: Generates human-readable explanations of policy
  decisions, including which rules fired, what evidence was considered,
  and what the outcome would have been under alternative inputs.
  Critical for auditability of automated trust decisions.
- retroactive_hardening.rs: Applies hardening constraints retroactively
  when a connector's trust level is downgraded, re-evaluating past
  decisions made under the higher trust level and flagging any that
  would not have been permitted under current policy.
- integrity_sweep_scheduler.rs: Periodic background sweep that verifies
  stored data integrity against expected checksums, scheduling repairs
  for any detected corruption via the repair subsystem.
- hardening_state_machine.rs: Minor fix to state transition guard.

Tools layer:
- counterfactual_replay.rs: Replays historical tool execution traces
  under modified policy configurations to answer "what would have
  happened if policy X had been in effect?" questions.
- replay_bundle.rs: Packages execution traces with their policy context
  into self-contained bundles for offline replay and analysis.
- repro_bundle_export.rs: Updated schema to v1 format with additional
  metadata fields for repair proof samples.

Repair subsystem (new):
- repair/mod.rs + repair/proof_carrying_decode.rs: Implements proof-
  carrying repair where each repair action carries a cryptographic
  proof that the repair is valid given the detected corruption pattern.
  Prevents repair mechanisms from being exploited to inject malicious
  data.

main.rs: Registers all new modules and wires up the integrity sweep
scheduler, policy explainer, and repair subsystem into the node
startup sequence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
